channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Industrial pollution in Gauteng linked to sulphur emissions affecting workers and communities
item.description: Residents across parts of Johannesburg have reported a strong 'rotten egg' smell in recent days, which the Association of Mineworkers and Construction Union (Amcu) says is linked to industrial air pol...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk released after Centre revokes National Security Act detention
item.description: New Delhi: Veteran climate activist Sonam Wangchuk was released from Jodhpur Jail on Saturday following the revocation of his detention under the National Security Act (NSA) by the Union government. W...

item.title: Oyo State Taskforce Impounds Nine Cattle to Protect Ijaye Farm Settlement
item.description: The Oyo State Rule of Law Enforcement Authority (OYRLEA) carried out an operation on Friday to impound nine cattle that were freely roaming and grazing inside Ijaye Farm Settlement, located in Akinyel...

item.title: Gordon Ramsay trains in the pool for upcoming Ironman event, inspired by son-in-law Adam Peaty
item.description: Celebrity chef Gordon Ramsay, aged 59, has been spotted training for an Ironman triathlon by focusing on swimming at a pool in the Toronto Athletic Club, Canada. He shared images on his Instagram Stor...

item.title: Halifax Council Approves Large-Scale Housing Development on Mic Mac Mall Parking Lots
item.description: Halifax has approved a major development that will transform the parking lots surrounding Mic Mac Mall in Dartmouth into a new neighbourhood designed for thousands of residents. The project, spearhead...

item.title: Campaigners urge NHS trusts to avoid adopting Palantir's Federated Data Platform over privacy and ethical concerns
item.description: A coalition of doctors, human rights organisations, and campaign groups, including Medact and Amnesty International, has called on NHS hospitals and trusts in England to reconsider or reject the use o...

item.title: Yanga and Azam FC go head-to-head in crucial NBC Premier League derby
item.description: Dar es Salaam football fans are gearing up for a big match at Benjamin Mkapa Stadium this Sunday, March 15, 2026, as league leaders Yanga take on Azam FC. Both teams are still unbeaten this season, ma...

item.title: Police Arrest Suspect in Murder of Witness D from Madlanga Commission
item.description: Police in Johannesburg have apprehended a suspect believed to have been involved in the killing of Marius 'Vlam' van der Merwe, known as Witness D at the Madlanga Commission. The arrest marks a signif...

item.title: Nigerian Army arrests two arms runners linked to Odonget violence in Cross River
item.description: Troops from the Nigerian Army, under the 13 Brigade, don arrest two people wey dem suspect say dem dey carry illegal arms for Odonget community, Obubra Local Government Area, Cross River State. The su...

item.title: Chancellor Rachel Reeves Plans Targeted Support Amid Rising Energy Costs
item.description: Chancellor Rachel Reeves is preparing targeted assistance for the most vulnerable households relying on heating oil, with an official announcement expected soon. Rural Labour MPs have highlighted the ...

item.title: Tolu Koula shines as Manly Sea Eagles narrowly lose to Canberra
item.description: During the recent NRL clash at 4 Pines Park, Manly Sea Eagles centre Tolu Koula demonstrated why he is considered a rising star in rugby league. With seven minutes remaining and Manly trailing Canberr...

item.title: Israeli airstrike kills 12 healthcare workers in southern Lebanon amid escalating conflict
item.description: An Israeli airstrike on Friday night destroyed a primary healthcare facility in Burj Qalaouiyah, southern Lebanon, killing 12 medical workers, including doctors, nurses, and paramedics. This brings th...

item.title: Two People Charged in Connection with Death of Burnaby Man
item.description: The Integrated Homicide Investigation Team (IHIT) has charged two individuals with first-degree murder following the death of 45-year-old Burnaby resident Masood Masjoody. Mehdi Ahmadzadeh Razavi, 48,...

item.title: Tanzania strengthens transport and maritime services to boost safety and development
item.description: DAR ES SALAAM: Tanzania government is stepping up efforts to modernize its transport infrastructure and maritime rescue services, aiming to enhance safety of people and property while supporting susta...

item.title: FCC chair warns broadcasters they risk losing licences over alleged misleading coverage of Iran conflict
item.description: Brendan Carr, the chair of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) under the Trump administration, has issued a stark warning to US broadcasters, suggesting that those disseminating what he descri...

item.title: Body of Mumbai-based seafarer to be repatriated next week after tanker attack
item.description: Mumbai resident Deonandan Prasad Singh, aged 54, who was serving as an additional chief engineer aboard the Marshall Islands-flagged oil tanker MT Safesea Vishnu, tragically lost his life when the ves...

item.title: Uzodimma Says Tinubu’s Economic Reforms Helped Nigeria Avoid Fuel Crisis
item.description: The Governor of Imo State, Hope Uzodimma, yarn say the economic reforms wey President Bola Ahmed Tinubu put for ground don help shield Nigeria from wahala wey fit happen for fuel sector because of the...

item.title: Baguio City records 9°C low amid ongoing amihan season
item.description: Baguio City residents woke up to a particularly chilly morning as the temperature plummeted to 9 degrees Celsius at 5 a.m., according to data from the Philippine Atmospheric, Geophysical and Astronomi...

item.title: Okoth Obado defends 900-acre land lease plan in Migori for community projects and mining
item.description: Former Migori Governor Okoth Obado has responded to allegations that he and his family are trying to unlawfully acquire 900 acres of land in Nyatike, Migori County, for mining and cotton farming. Obad...

item.title: Long COVID Continues to Impact Thousands in Los Angeles County with Chronic Illness and Disability
item.description: As Los Angeles County moves on from the acute phase of the COVID-19 pandemic, thousands of residents continue to suffer from long COVID, a chronic condition that manifests in fatigue, cognitive diffic...
